steps.title: Identify the text's structure
steps.content: The text opens with Lönnstedt's 2013 finding that ocean acidification affects fish behavior, then immediately pivots with “However” to flag a methodological problem: the study used only about 28 fish. Clements' 2022 review cautions that a small sample size increases the risk of biased analysis and exaggerated effects. The text presents a finding, then raises a concern about its reliability.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘To note a potential concern about the findings of a scientific study’: Matches exactly — the “However” pivot introduces a methodological critique of Lönnstedt's study. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘To discuss an aspect of ocean acidification that is frequently overlooked’: The text does not claim any aspect of ocean acidification is overlooked; it questions a study's sample size, not the breadth of the topic. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘To present a debate between two research teams about a cause of ocean acidification’: No debate about causes appears; Clements critiques Lönnstedt's method, not a cause of acidification. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘To explain how the behavior of a fish species has changed over time’: The text reports a behavioral finding from one study and then questions its reliability — it does not track behavioral change over time. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
